findings.evidence: Line 131 isHomeomorph_total' : IsHomeomorph fun p => (p.1, toFun p); a homeomorphism preserving the first coordinate is fiberwise a homeomorphism. Only use of the field (line 173) goes through `.isEmbedding`, obtainable from the total map.
findings.fix: Drop the isHomeomorph_apply' field; expose it as a lemma `AmbientIsotopy.isHomeomorph_apply` proved from isHomeomorph_total'. Update `refl` and `isotopy`'s `prop'` accordingly.
findings.issue: AmbientIsotopy.isHomeomorph_apply' (each slice is a self-homeomorphism) is strictly weaker than and derivable from isHomeomorph_total': the total homeomorphism preserves the I-coordinate, so it restricts to a homeomorphism on each fiber {t}×Y. Storing it as a structure field forces every constructor to discharge a redundant obligation.

findings.evidence: isEmbedding_apply (line 68) is currently F.prop' t but is a consequence of isEmbedding_total (line 64); the refl/symm proofs do not rely on the HomotopyWith predicate machinery for the embedding content.
findings.fix: Extend plain `Homotopy f₀ f₁` plus isEmbedding_total', and derive `isEmbedding_apply` from the total map instead of storing it via prop'.
findings.issue: Isotopy extends `HomotopyWith f₀ f₁ IsEmbedding` (carrying prop': every slice an embedding) on top of the strictly stronger isEmbedding_total'. Slice embeddings follow from the total embedding (restriction of an embedding to {t}×X), so the predicate-homotopy is redundant data.

summary: Definitions sit at the right (maximally general, continuous) level with no unused or over-strong theorem hypotheses, but both structures store a derivable consequence as a required field, over-specifying the interface that all downstream isotopy layers must construct.
